title: mercurialis perennis subvar. sylvatica
intro: Mercurialis perennis subvar. sylvatica, also known as the Woodland Dog's Mercury, is a subvariety of the Dog's Mercury plant, a perennial herb native to Europe. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to aid in reducing swelling and fighting free radicals in the body. The health benefits of Mercurialis perennis subvar. sylvatica include its ability to relieve arthritis and joint pain, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering the leaves to be used in capsules or tinctures.
